title: 1.1: Identifies the runtime structure of the signed session ID cookie value (e.g., UUID-like left part plus a signature)
1.2: Reports the Redis TTL difference between authenticated and non-authenticated sessions using observed values (e.g., 300 seconds vs 604800 seconds)
1.3: States the Redis session key naming pattern (e.g., session:<uuid>)
1.4: Demonstrates logout-like purge behavior that deletes the current Redis key (e.g., after_purge_key_exists=False)
1.5: States next-load behavior after corrupting stored session bytes (e.g., loaded_session_keys_after_corruption=[]).
1.6: States the HTTP-level outcome when stored session bytes are malformed (e.g., after_corrupt_status=200 rather than a 5xx error).
1.7: Identifies the deserialization boundary as pickle.loads on Redis bytes.
1.8: Reports the “cannot forge signed session ID” constraint using runtime observations (e.g., tamper_exception=BadSignature or tampered_cookie_keys=[]).
